settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Manage tracked keywords and the monitoring scheduler. Defaults: every 6 hours, single keyword.

help.title: How this works
help.subtitle: Category definitions, classifier signals, and monitoring limits.

intro.title: Purpose
intro.body: Periodic monitoring of Google's top-10 SERP for a branded query (default: 'starcasino', geo Netherlands). Each domain is classified by combining SERP signals with page-scrape signals. Results are persisted as snapshots — the History chart shows the category distribution over time.

categories.title: Categories
categories.affiliate: A partner site. Ranks on the brand query and routes traffic to the official site with an affiliate tag (btag, aff_id). Monetises via brand-side conversions.
categories.thief: A brand-theft site. Ranks on the brand query, but the primary CTA leads to a competing casino (Jack's, TonyBet, Holland Casino). Intercepts brand traffic to a third party's benefit.
categories.unclear: Insufficient signal to classify. Typically: informational pages (Wikipedia, Trustpilot, Google Play) or sites behind bot protection that returned no useful content.

signals.title: Classifier signals
signals.intro: The classifier combines deterministic rules with an LLM verdict (Claude Opus 4.7 via OpenRouter). Signals split into two groups by whether they require a successful page scrape.
signals.serpBody: Derived from SERP data — independent of page availability.

serpItems.0: Page domain matches the brand domain
serpItems.1: Page domain matches a known competitor (jacks.nl, tonybet.nl, …)
serpItems.2: Domain contains the brand stem (typosquat — starcasino-nl.com etc.)
serpItems.3: Page title matches a review or listicle pattern (review, top N, beste, alternatieven)

signals.pageBody: More precise but requires the page to load. Bot protection (Cloudflare, DataDome) zeroes these out.

pageItems.2: Brand mention count in the page body

signals.fusion: Signals accumulate into per-category scores. The highest-scoring category wins; below threshold → 'unclear'. The LLM verdict is weighted alongside rules (60/40), except when the LLM returns 'unclear' at low confidence — rules then act alone.

unclear.title: Passive-scrape limitation
unclear.body1: The decisive signal for distinguishing 'affiliate' from 'brand thief' is the final domain in the redirect chain after clicking the primary CTA. `final == starcasino.nl + btag=…` → affiliate; `final == jacks.nl + aff_id=…` → thief.
unclear.body2: Many casino sites are bot-protected. A headless browser receives a challenge page without CTA content, or CTAs are injected by JS after page load. As a result a portion of results remains in 'unclear'.
unclear.calloutBody: Active probing — an isolated browser context that clicks the CTA, awaits redirects, and records the final URL. Out of scope for the current prototype; documented in `wiki/synthesis/phase-plan`.

monitoring.title: Check schedule
monitoring.body: The scheduler runs analysis on a cron expression. Default: every 4 hours (180 runs/month, within SerpAPI's free tier of 250/month). Settings let you change the interval, trigger a one-off check, or pause the schedule. Each run is persisted as a snapshot with a unique ID.

stack.title: Technology stack
stack.body: Backend: Node.js 20, TypeScript, Fastify, Playwright (Chromium), better-sqlite3, OpenRouter API. Frontend: React, Vite, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, Recharts. Infrastructure: Docker Compose (web + worker), Caddy reverse proxy with DNS-01 Let's Encrypt, Cloudflare (DNS-only).

keyword.title: Tracked keywords
keyword.hint: Each keyword is monitored on every scheduler tick. Switch which one the dashboard shows from the header.
keyword.cannotDeleteLast: Cannot delete the last remaining keyword

enrichment.unavailable: Enrichment data not yet collected — re-run analyze.
